description: Question sets for Kitchen Lab. Covers science inquiry skills, material properties, states of matter, mixing and reactions, the water cycle, forces, soil, habitats, and light and sound. The game engine presents virtual experiments — the child predicts, observes, and records outcomes. Question types lean heavily on predict_observe_record and drag_and_drop to mirror the hands-on inquiry model in the Ontario Science curriculum.

progression_rules.note: Science questions should be presented in inquiry sequence where possible: predict → observe → explain. The predict_observe_record mechanic in the game engine handles this as a three-step interaction on a single question_id.

question_sets.instructions: First: tap what you think will happen. Then: watch the experiment. Then: tap what you saw.

questions.experiment: Drop a heavy rock and a feather at the same time.
observe_options: They landed at the same time.

questions.experiment: Put an ice cube on a warm plate.
questions.predict_prompt: What will happen to the ice cube?
questions.observe_prompt: What happened to the ice cube?
explain_options: The heat from the plate made it melt.
questions.correct_explanation: The heat from the plate made it melt.

questions.experiment: Shine a torch at a wall in a dark room.
questions.predict_prompt: What will you see on the wall?
observe_options: A bright spot of light appeared.
questions.correct_observation: A bright spot of light appeared.
questions.explain_prompt: Where did the light come from?

question_sets.instructions: Drag each item into the LIVING or NON-LIVING bin.

questions.prompt: Sort these things into Living or Non-Living.
Is a mushroom living or non-living? Why?
distractors: Living — it is made of plastic.

question_sets.instructions: After the experiment, choose the best way to record what you found.

questions.experiment: Plant one seed with water and one seed without water for a week.
questions.observe_prompt: After one week, what did you observe?
observe_options: The plant with water grew taller.
The plant without water grew taller.
questions.correct_observation: The plant with water grew taller.
questions.record_prompt: What is the best way to record this finding?
record_options: Draw both plants and label which had water.
Just remember it in my head.
Tell a friend but don't write it down.
questions.correct_record: Draw both plants and label which had water.

questions.prompt: A scientist finishes an experiment. What should she do FIRST to communicate her findings?
questions.answer: Write down or draw what she observed.
distractors: Tell everyone the experiment was fun.
Clean up and forget what happened.
Start a new experiment right away.

question_sets.instructions: Tap the correct form of energy.

questions.prompt: A lamp lights up a room. What type of energy is this?
A drum makes a loud bang. What type of energy is this?
A campfire warms your hands. What type of energy is this?
A spinning top spins across the floor. What type of energy?
Sort these into the correct energy type.

question_sets.instructions: Drag each material into the correct property bin.

questions.prompt: Sort these materials: Which are HARD and which are SOFT?
Which material would be BEST for making a raincoat? Why?
questions.answer: Waterproof plastic — it does not let water through.
distractors: Cotton fabric — it is soft and comfortable.
Paper — it is easy to fold.
Foam — it is light and flexible.

questions.prompt: Which material is ABSORBENT?
Which material is FLEXIBLE (can bend without breaking)?

question_sets.instructions: Is it a liquid or a solid? Tap your answer.

questions.prompt: Orange juice
A wooden block
Honey
An ice cube
Shampoo
A chocolate bar
What is the main difference between a liquid and a solid?
questions.answer: A liquid flows and takes the shape of its container. A solid keeps its own shape.
distractors: A liquid is always cold. A solid is always warm.
A liquid is blue. A solid is brown.
A liquid can be touched. A solid cannot.

questions.experiment: Put a tray of liquid water in the freezer.
questions.predict_prompt: What will happen to the water?
questions.observe_prompt: After two hours, what do you see?
questions.explain_prompt: What caused the water to freeze?
explain_options: The cold temperature removed heat from the water.
questions.correct_explanation: The cold temperature removed heat from the water.

questions.prompt: At what temperature does water freeze?
You leave an ice cream cone outside on a hot day. What happens?
questions.answer: It melts — the heat changes the solid to a liquid.

questions.experiment: Add salt to water and stir.
questions.predict_prompt: What will happen to the salt?
predict_options: It will dissolve — you won't be able to see it.
It will turn the water red.
questions.correct_prediction: It will dissolve — you won't be able to see it.
questions.observe_prompt: After stirring, what do you see?
observe_options: The salt dissolved and the water looks clear.
The salt is still sitting at the bottom.
questions.correct_observation: The salt dissolved and the water looks clear.
questions.explain_prompt: What is the mixture of salt dissolved in water called?

questions.experiment: Add sand to water and stir.
predict_options: It will stay mixed for a bit, then settle at the bottom.
It will turn the water blue.
questions.correct_prediction: It will stay mixed for a bit, then settle at the bottom.
questions.observe_prompt: After waiting, what do you see?
observe_options: Sand settled at the bottom — it did not dissolve.
questions.correct_observation: Sand settled at the bottom — it did not dissolve.
questions.explain_prompt: Why is a sand-water mixture different from salt water?
explain_options: Sand does not dissolve — it forms a suspension, not a solution.
questions.correct_explanation: Sand does not dissolve — it forms a suspension, not a solution.

questions.prompt: Sort these mixtures: Which are SOLUTIONS (dissolved completely) and which are SUSPENSIONS (do not dissolve)?

question_sets.instructions: Tap the correct meaning of the safety symbol shown.

questions.prompt: What does this symbol mean?
What does this symbol mean?
What does this symbol mean?
What does this symbol tell you to do?
You see a skull-and-crossbones symbol on a bottle under the kitchen sink. What should you do?
questions.answer: Do not touch it — tell an adult.
distractors: Taste a little bit to see what it is.

question_sets.instructions: Drag the stages of the water cycle into the correct order.

questions.prompt: Put the water cycle stages in order, starting from the sun heating a lake.

descriptions.evaporation: Water heats up and becomes water vapour that rises into the air.
descriptions.condensation: Water vapour cools and forms clouds.
descriptions.precipitation: Water falls from clouds as rain, snow, or hail.
descriptions.collection: Water collects in lakes, rivers, and oceans.

questions.prompt: What happens to water when the sun heats it?
What causes clouds to form?
What is precipitation?
questions.answer: Water falling from clouds as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

questions.prompt: Ice is water in what state?
Steam is water in what state?
A puddle of water is in what state?
Sort these into the correct state of water.
Sort these into the components found IN soil.
What is HUMUS?
questions.answer: Decomposed plant and animal material that makes soil rich in nutrients.

questions.prompt: Why do gardeners add compost to soil?
questions.answer: To add nutrients that help plants grow.
distractors: To remove water from the soil.
To make the soil a different colour.

questions.experiment: Mix sandy soil, clay, and dead leaves together.
questions.predict_prompt: What type of soil are you making?
predict_options: Loam — a rich mixture good for growing plants.
Pure sand — good for desert plants.
Pure clay — too dense for most plants.
questions.correct_prediction: Loam — a rich mixture good for growing plants.

questions.prompt: You kick a ball. What type of force is this?
A magnet attracts a paperclip toward it. What force?
A dropped ball falls to the ground. What force caused this?
You slide a book across a rough table and it slows down. What force slowed it?
Sort these actions into PUSH or PULL.
Sort these organisms into PRODUCER, CONSUMER, or DECOMPOSER.
What do PRODUCERS use to make their own food?
What role do DECOMPOSERS play in an ecosystem?
questions.answer: They break down dead organisms and return nutrients to the soil.

questions.prompt: In this food chain: Grass → Rabbit → Fox, what is the RABBIT?
questions.answer: A consumer (it eats the grass and is eaten by the fox).

questions.prompt: A food web is different from a food chain because...
questions.answer: A food web shows that most organisms eat and are eaten by many different organisms, not just one.
distractors: A food web is always shorter than a food chain.
A food web only includes plants.
A food web shows only decomposers.

questions.prompt: If rabbits were removed from a food web that includes: Grass → Rabbit → Fox and Grass → Rabbit → Hawk, what would likely happen to the fox?
questions.answer: The fox population would decrease because its food source is gone.
distractors: The fox would eat more grass.

questions.prompt: What does the ARROW mean in a food chain? (e.g. Grass → Rabbit)
questions.answer: Energy flows from the grass to the rabbit — the rabbit eats the grass.
distractors: The grass is bigger than the rabbit.
The rabbit and grass are the same organism.

questions.experiment: Shine a torch at a mirror.
questions.predict_prompt: What will happen to the light beam?
predict_options: It will bounce off the mirror at an angle.
questions.correct_prediction: It will bounce off the mirror at an angle.
observe_options: The light reflected off the mirror.
The mirror absorbed all the light.
The light bent through the mirror.
questions.correct_observation: The light reflected off the mirror.
questions.explain_prompt: What is the bouncing of light off a surface called?

questions.experiment: Shine a torch through a glass of water onto a white wall.
questions.predict_prompt: What will you see on the wall?
predict_options: The light will bend and the beam will change direction.
questions.correct_prediction: The light will bend and the beam will change direction.
observe_options: It bent as it passed through the water — refraction.
It came out the same direction.
questions.correct_observation: It bent as it passed through the water — refraction.
questions.explain_prompt: What is the bending of light as it passes through a different material called?

questions.prompt: Sort these materials: which let light THROUGH and which BLOCK light?

questions.experiment: Pluck a thick rubber band, then a thin rubber band.
questions.predict_prompt: Which rubber band will make a higher pitch sound?
observe_options: The thin band made a higher pitch sound.
The thick band made a higher pitch sound.
questions.correct_observation: The thin band made a higher pitch sound.
questions.explain_prompt: Why does the thin band have a higher pitch?
explain_options: It vibrates faster, creating more frequent sound waves.
questions.correct_explanation: It vibrates faster, creating more frequent sound waves.

questions.prompt: What causes sound?
questions.answer: Vibrations — objects moving back and forth rapidly.

questions.prompt: How is a loud sound different from a soft sound?
questions.answer: A loud sound has a greater amplitude (bigger vibrations).
distractors: A loud sound has a higher pitch.
A loud sound only travels in water.

questions.prompt: How is a high-pitched sound different from a low-pitched sound?
questions.answer: A high pitch has faster vibrations (higher frequency).
distractors: A high pitch is always louder.
A high pitch is made only by big objects.
